comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
I would like to discuss whether we want to support #2826. This adds a support burden, that I believe is better handled in systemd.

Our original mandate was Docker 1.12 compatability. We've done that in other places it would create a burden (volumes, for example). I don't see this as serious added complexity; it was two hours of dev time and around two hundred lines of code to implement. It's a lot less complex than our remote client work, for example.
My concern is long term support and opening up pandora's box of what it means for process management.

We're not really managing processes - we trigger a standard container restart if we detect the container exited. It's all things that Podman already does, just with a bit of plumbing to tell it when to do them.

I believe this opens a whole can of worms, that is better handled by systemd.  I believe it would be better for our customers in the long run to use systemd for this, and for us to document it.   We should discuss this at next meeting.  BTW I think we would need to handle this type of stuff in PODs as well.
